specification_date: Extract the specification date from the header. Common patterns: 'SPECIFICATION No <num> dd <date>' / 'СПЕЦИФИКАЦИЯ № <num> от <date>'. The date follows 'dd' or 'от'. Example: 'СПЕЦИФИКАЦИЯ № 160 от 26.03.2025'. DO NOT confuse with contract_date which appears in the same header block alongside contract_no. Keep the exact format from the document and add to specification_date.
contract_no: Extract the contract number that this specification refers to. Labels: 'Contract No' / 'Contract No.' / 'К КОНТРАКТУ №' / 'CONTRACT'. Examples: 'SPRMO22', '220521', 'TIMEMO25'. Write only the number (no date or counterparty). Add to contract_no.
additional_agreements: Extract addendum references if mentioned. Labels: 'Addendum' / 'Supplementary Agreement' / 'DS No' / 'Дополнительное соглашение'. Include numbers and dates if present. If multiple, join with '; '. If absent, leave empty. Add to additional_agreements.
buyer: Identify the buyer. Labels: 'Buyer' / 'Customer' / 'Consignee' / 'Покупатель'. Keep the full legal name (and address if shown) in one line. Add to buyer.
seller: Identify the seller. Labels: 'Seller' / 'Supplier' / 'Продавец'. Keep the full legal name (and address if shown) in one line. Add to seller.
incoterms: Extract Incoterms — three-letter code and place. Codes may appear in Latin (EXW/FOB/CFR/CIF/DAP) or transliterated Cyrillic (ФОБ = FOB, ДАП = DAP, СИФ = CIF, СФР = CFR). Examples: 'ФОБ Циндао, Китай', 'DAP Москва', 'FOB Dalian'. Keep the wording exactly as in the document. Add to incoterms.
terms_of_payment: Extract payment terms. Labels: 'Terms of payment' / 'Payment' / 'Payment terms' / 'Условия оплаты'. Keep the full wording (e.g., '100% payment before arriving at coldstore', '20% advance, 10% after inspection, 70% balance against scanned copies'). Add to terms_of_payment.
total_price: Extract the overall total. Labels: 'Total' / 'Всего / Total' / 'Сумма' / 'Amount'. Usually in the bottom row of the goods table with currency. Examples: '990 604,79 RMB', '82 402,19 USD', '225 000,00 RMB'. If amount in words is also shown, do not replace the numeric total. Keep currency and number format as in the document. Add to total_price.
HS_code: Extract HS/tariff code(s). Labels: 'HS CODE' / 'HS Code' / 'Tariff code' / 'CUSTOMS CODE'. Format may vary: 6 digits with dot ('0304.81'), 10 digits without separators ('0303899007'), or with spaces. Often absent in specifications — if not shown, leave empty. If multiple, join with '; '. Add to HS_code.
country_of_origin: Extract origin country if explicitly labeled. Labels: 'Country of origin' / 'Origin' / 'Страна происхождения'. Example: 'CHILE'. If no explicit field, leave empty (do not infer from seller's country or other context). Add to country_of_origin.
products: The 'products' block is expandable: create product_1, product_2, ... for each line item in the table and keep order. For each position fill all available fields. If an attribute is given once in a shared block (e.g., one Latin species for all positions), duplicate it in the relevant product_i. In token_refs include references to the source fragments/cells used.
products[*].name_product: Extract the commercial name from the goods column. Headers: 'Description of goods' / 'Product' / 'Товар' / 'Goods'. If multi-line, join into one line. Add to products[i].name_product.
products[*].latin_name: Extract the Latin species name, typically in parentheses after the commercial name (e.g., 'LEPIDOCYBIUM FLAVOBRUNNEUM', 'ONCORHYNCHUS MYKISS', 'Undaria pinnatifida'). Note: not every parenthesized text is Latin — for example '(ЭСКОЛАР)' is a Russian/local name in Cyrillic, not Latin. Pick only the scientific species name. If absent, leave empty. Add to products[i].latin_name.
products[*].size_product: Extract size/grade/pack. Examples: '1-3 kg', '3-6 kg', '800-1200 g', '10kg (10×1kg)', '3-4 LB (1.3-1.8 KG)', '80/120'. Keep the wording as in the document. Add to products[i].size_product.
products[*].unit_box: Extract the accounting/pricing unit if explicitly shown. Labels: 'per carton' / 'per kg' / 'unit' / 'CASE' / 'bag' / 'ctn' / 'мешок' / 'коробка' / 'кор.'. Add to products[i].unit_box. If not shown, leave empty.
products[*].packages: Extract number of packages. Labels: 'Packages' / 'Cases' / 'Cartons' / 'No. of packages' / 'Quantity, ctns' / 'Quantity, bags' / 'Кол-во мест' / 'Кол-во кор.' / 'Кол-во мешков'. If type is shown, keep both (e.g., '1153 BAGS', '738 CTNS', '2500 cartons'). Add to products[i].packages.
products[*].net_weight: Extract net weight per item if a weight column is present. Labels: 'Net weight' / 'NET KG' / 'Вес нетто' / 'кг'. Specifications often DO NOT include separate net weight columns — if no such column exists, leave empty. Keep number and unit as in the document (dot/comma). Add to products[i].net_weight.
products[*].gross_weight: Extract gross weight per item. Labels: 'Gross weight' / 'G.W.' / 'Вес брутто'. Often absent in specifications — if not shown, leave empty. Keep number and unit as in the document. Add to products[i].gross_weight.
products[*].price_per_unit: Extract unit price. Labels: 'Unit price' / 'Price per ...' / 'Цена за мешок' / 'Цена за коробку' / 'Цена за кор.' / 'Цена за меш.'. The unit usually matches what is being sold by (bag/carton/kg). Keep currency and number format exactly as in the document. Add to products[i].price_per_unit.
products[*].total_price: Extract the line total. Labels: 'Line total' / 'Amount' / 'Сумма' / 'Итого'. If no explicit value, compute as quantity/weight × price_per_unit, keeping currency and format. Add to products[i].total_price.
